name: Dana Whitfield
content: This is Dana Whitfield at the Northwind Reno fulfillment center. Arm 3 in our pilot zone keeps throwing calibration faults — second day in a row.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Thanks Dana. Arm 3's FleetView fault log shows repeated wrist-encoder calibration errors, which on a new install usually means the encoder shipped miscalibrated rather than a mechanical fault.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: The floor crew assumed mechanical — it drops small items intermittently. Do they need to pull the wrist assembly?

name: MeridianAssistant
content: No — please don't open the wrist. Meridian can push a firmware recalibration remotely this afternoon; the arm needs to be idle for about ten minutes while it runs.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: Arm 3 is idle now, go ahead.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Recalibration pushed and verified — encoder readings are back in spec. Keep an eye on FleetView fault trends for 48 hours; it should stay clean.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: Two days clean, and pick accuracy on Arm 3 matches the other three arms. Closing this one out.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: Follow-up on our pilot setup: I have two more shift leads who need FleetView access for the Reno small-parts zone.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Easy one. Send their work emails and I'll add them to Northwind's FleetView Team subscription with the shift-lead dashboard as their landing page.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: Sent to your provisioning address just now. While you're in there — can shift leads see per-arm utilization? That's the view I use for lane assignments.

name: MeridianAssistant
content: Yes — per-arm utilization is included in the shift-lead view. Both accounts are provisioned; they'll get invites within the hour.

name: Dana Whitfield
content: Both are in and can see the utilization panel. That's everything for this week, thanks.
